spec_match.rationale: True A/B: deprecated `inset-area` on the left panel, new `position-area` on the right, shared direction value. Demonstrates the rename's behaviour-preserving promise.

interactive.rationale: Direction <select> drives both code labels and live computed-style readouts; the replacement panel visibly moves for each selected value.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Card promises 'A/B with same anchor, popover, direction values' — page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: The page now reports requested vs computed values and measured anchor offsets, making the removed legacy spelling and working replacement explicit for each direction.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables; .tag-dep / .tag-new both set background and color together.

summary: Re-tested with chrome-devtools-mcp in Chrome 148. Before the fix, the select changed both code labels, but Chrome did not recognise `inset-area`, so the legacy panel had no inline style, computed `position-area: none`, and stayed at a static -100px / -80px offset while the replacement panel moved. After the fix, the page exposes this directly: every direction option updates both requested labels and readouts; the deprecated panel reports `computed: none`, while the replacement panel computes `top`, `bottom`, `left`, `right`, `left top`, and `right bottom` with matching measured offsets. The feature conformance route reported 6 PASS / 0 FAIL with no console errors.
